comparisonDesign: one instruction-1.7.3 managed-interface run using the two-tool coding-agent surface for a runtime-bound implementation named only by inspected source
sourceRetention: source-free metrics and normalized MCP tool names, relationships, and anchors only; no source, prompts, MCP payloads, diffs, raw commands, or free-form model text

tuningHistory.reason: The agent correctly classified the injected binding as an implementation, while the first fixture version expected a dependency relationship; behavior remained incomplete because the target was unresolved.
Behavior and exact edit scope passed, but the agent bypassed refinement with text search, so managed-interface adoption failed.
The agent used the compact packet first, requested one exact source-target implementation delta, consumed the serializer, and passed public and hidden behavior checks.

learnings: Known deterministic symbolic targets belong in the initial packet; runtime-bound implementations whose paths remain unknown belong behind bounded refinement.
An injected concrete binding is most naturally requested as an implementation relationship from the consuming publisher.
The advertised source-target resolver should require an exact identifier exposed by inspected source; generic refinement invites unnecessary expansion.
The accepted run used exactly one source-target call and changed only the service, runtime serializer, and regression test required by the task.
